metadata.summary: A collection of various deep-learning and data handling tools for running and building deep-learning models for computational biology.

documentation:
# deepbio-toolkit

A collection of various deep-learning and data handling tools for running and building deep-learning models for computational biology.

## Installation

The DBTK can be installed via pip:

```bash
pip install deepbio-toolkit
```

functions.name: dbtk._utils.export
documentation:
Use a decorator to avoid retyping function/class names.

* Based on an idea by Duncan Booth:
  http://groups.google.com/group/comp.lang.python/msg/11cbb03e09611b8a
* Improved via a suggestion by Dave Angel:
  http://groups.google.com/group/comp.lang.python/msg/3d400fb22d8a42e1

documentation: An indexable memory-mapped interface for FASTA files.

classes.name: dbtk.data.formats.microstore.MicroStore

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.microstore.MicroStore.close
documentation: Close the MicroStore and release resources.

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.microstore.MicroStore._bisect
documentation:
Get the current index of a key.

Args:
    key (str): The key to lookup.

Raises:
    KeyError: If the key is not found.

Returns:
    int: The index of the key.

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.microstore.MicroStore._compress
documentation:
Compress the given data using the deflate algorithm.

Args:
    data (bytes): The data to compress.

Returns:
    bytes: The compressed data.

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.microstore.MicroStore._decompress
documentation:
Decompress the given data using the deflate algorithm.

Args:
    data (bytes): The data to decompress.

Returns:
    bytes: The decompressed data.

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.microstore.MicroStore._hash
documentation:
Compute the SHA1 hash of the given data.

Args:
    data (bytes): The data to hash.

Returns:
    bytes: The hash of the data.

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.microstore.MicroStore.key
documentation:
Get the key associated with an index.

Args:
    index (int): The index to lookup.

Raises:
    KeyError: If the index is out of bounds.

Returns:
    bytes: The key associated with the index.

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.microstore.MicroStore.get
documentation:
Get the value associated with a key or index.

Args:
    key_or_index (Union[int, bytes]): The key or index to lookup.

Raises:
    KeyError: If the key is not found.
    KeyError: If the index is out of bounds.
    TypeError: If the key type is not bytes.

Returns:
    bytes: The value associated with the key or index.

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.microstore.MicroStore._to_insert
documentation:
Filter out duplicates and compute insertion points for the given keys and values.

Args:
    keys_and_values (Iterable[Tuple[bytes, bytes]]): The keys and values to insert.

Returns:
    Dict[bytes, Tuple[int, bytes, bytes]]: A dictionary of insertion points.

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.microstore.MicroStore._insert
documentation:
[Unsafe] Insert the given keys and values into the MicroStore.

Args:
    to_insert (Dict[bytes, Tuple[int, bytes, bytes]]): The keys and values to insert.
    show_progress (bool, optional): Show progress during insertion. Defaults to False.

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.microstore.MicroStore.write
documentation:
Write a key/value pair to the MicroStore.

Args:
    key (bytes): The key to write.
    value (bytes): The value to write.

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.microstore.MicroStore.batch_write
documentation:
Write multiple key/value pairs to the MicroStore.

Args:
    keys_and_values (Iterable[Tuple[bytes, bytes]]): The keys and values to write.
    show_progress (bool, optional): Show progress during insertion. Defaults to False.
A small key-value store that stores values in a sorted array on disk with fast access via memory-mapping.

modules.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old
documentation:
The SequenceStore is a custom binary format for efficiently storing sequence data with fast read
access.

Mode 1: sequence store without samples/abundance information
Mode 2: sequence store with samples/abundance information

- Sequence IDs are optional. If none are provided, they will not be stored and will be generated
automatically upon access based on the sample name information. If provided, they must be unique
and must be present for all input sequences.

1. Write all sequences to the store.

functions.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.encode_string
documentation:
Encode a string using huffman codes.

Args:
    string (str): The string to encode
    huffman_codes (Dict[str, bitarray]): The huffman codes

Returns:
    bytes: The encoded string

functions.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.decode_string
documentation:
Decode a huffman encoded string.

Args:
    encoded (bytes): The encoded string
    huffman_codes (Dict[str, bitarray]): The huffman codes

Returns:
    str: The decoded string

functions.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.uint_size
documentation:
Calculate the number of bits required to store an unsigned integer.

Args:
    n (int): The integer to store

Returns:
    int: The unsigned integer size in bits

functions.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.uint
documentation:
Create a numpy dtype for an unsigned integer with a specific number of bits.

Args:
    bit_count (int): The number of bits for the unsigned integer

Returns:
    np.dtype: The numpy dtype for the unsigned integer

documentation:
Create a new SequenceStore.

Args:
    path (Union[str, Path]): The path to the sequence store file
    mode (int, optional): The mode of the sequence store. Defaults to 1.

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.SequenceStore.load
documentation: Load the sequence store from disk.

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.SequenceStore.close
documentation: Close the sequence store.

documentation:
Check if the sequence store has sequence IDs.

Returns:
    bool: True if the sequence store has sequence IDs

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.SequenceStore.lookup
documentation:
Look up the index of a sequence ID.

Args:
    sequence_id (str): The sequence ID

Raises:
    KeyError: If the sequence ID is not found
    ValueError: If the store does not store sequence IDs

Returns:
    int: The index of the sequence ID

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.SequenceStore.sequence
documentation:
Get a sequence by sequence ID.

Args:
    identifier (str): The sequence ID

Raises:
    KeyError: If the sequence ID is not found
    ValueError: If the store does not store sequence IDs

Returns:
    str: The sequence

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.SequenceStore.__len__
documentation:
Get the number of sequences in the store.

Returns:
    int: The number of sequences

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.SequenceStore.__getitem__
documentation:
Get a sequence by index or sequence ID.

Args:
    i (Union[str, int]): The index or sequence ID

Returns:
    str: The sequence

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.SequenceStore.__getitem__
documentation:
Get an iterator over sequences by slice or indices

Args:
    i (Union[slice, np.ndarray]): The slice or indices

Yields:
    Generator[str, None, None]: The sequence

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.SequenceStore.__iter__
documentation:
Iterate over all sequences in the store.

Yields:
    Generator[str, None, None]: The sequence

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.SequenceStore.SequenceWriter.__init__
documentation:
Create a new SequenceWriter.

Args:
    path (Union[str, Path]): The path to the sequence store file
    sequence_id_bucket_ratio (float, optional): The bucket/key ratio for the sequence IDs hash table . Defaults to 0.1.
    show_progress (bool, optional): Show progress during writing. Defaults to False.

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.SequenceStore.SequenceWriter.write
documentation:
Write a sequence to the store.

Args:
    sequence (str): The sequence to write
    sequence_id (Optional[str], optional): The corresponding sequence identifier. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    int: The index of the inserted sequence

methods.name: dbtk.data.formats.sequence_store_old.SequenceStore.SequenceWriter.finish
documentation: Write the sequences to the store on disk.
A class for storing sequences in a custom binary format for efficient read access.

methods.name: dbtk.loggers.wandb.WandbLogger.save_dir
documentation:
This corrects the implementation of `save_dir` in `WandbLogger` to
return the directory of the experiment instead of the directory of the
logger.

classes.name: dbtk.nn.models.taxonomy.TopDownTaxonomyClassificationModel

methods.name: dbtk.nn.models.taxonomy.TopDownTaxonomyClassificationModel._forward_taxonomy
documentation: Top-down taxonomy prediction

The top-down taxonomy architecture proposed by SetBERT.

encoder: The model used to embed DNA sequences.
embed_dim: The dimension of the embedded DNA sequences.
taxonomy_tree: The taxonomy tree used to guide the prediction.

methods.name: dbtk.nn.models.contrastive.ContrastivePretrainingModel.__init__
documentation:
Create a contrastive pretraining model.

Args:
    encoder_a (nn.Module): The first encoder.
    encoder_b (nn.Module): The second encoder.
    embed_dim_a (int): The dimensionality of the first encoder embeddings.
    embed_dim_b (int): The dimensionality of the second encoder embeddings.
    projection_dim (Optional[int], optional): The dimensionality to project embeddings to before comparing. Defaults to smallest of `embed_dim_a` and `embed_dim_b`.
    shared_projections (Optional[bool], optional): Use shared projection weights. Defaults to False.
    max_temperature (float, optional): The maximum temperature value. Defaults to 100.0.
